column1: # Per-module Go coverage FLOORS — the no-regression ratchet.
# check-coverage-ratchet.sh fails a backend matrix job if its module's
# `go tool cover` total drops below the floor here.
# Floors are set a few pp BELOW the measured baseline so normal CI/local
# variance (race+atomic mode, CGO on user-service/local-storage) does not
# false-fail. They are a FLOOR, not a target: ratchet them UP as coverage
# climbs (per the coverage-cadence policy — pure-logic surfaces first, no
# mock teatro). Lowering a floor requires a written justification in the
# PR (an intentional, explained regression — e.g. deleting a tested file).
# Format: <service><TAB><floor-percent>
# Measured 2026-05-25 (local, non-race): common 33.0, gateway 40.7,
# core 8.3, app-management 14.8, message-bus 12.9, local-storage 7.0,
# user-service 3.9. `pkg` has no floor yet (advisory until measured in CI).
common
gateway
core
app-management
message-bus
local-storage
user-service
